pattern: Since rearrangement is free, only the count of odd-frequency letters in a range matters, and prefix parity bitmasks answer that count per query in constant time.
hints: Because you may rearrange the substring, its exact letters do not matter, only how many letters appear an odd number of times within the range.
Pairs of equal letters cost nothing to mirror; each replacement can fix two odd letters at once, so you need at most floor(odd_count / 2) replacements.
Track a 26-bit parity prefix where bit c flips on each occurrence; XOR of two prefixes gives the parity inside a range, and popcount/2 compared to k answers each query.

edge_inputs.description: mixed queries including a single character
already a palindrome needs zero replacements
full range with zero budget but odd letters
exactly enough budget to fix all odd letters

stress.description: long string and many random ranges with varied budgets
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('abcde') for _ in range(n))
    q = rng.randint(1, min(2000, n))
    queries = []
    for _ in range(q):
        a = rng.randrange(0, n)
        b = rng.randrange(0, n)
        if a > b:
            a, b = b, a
        k = rng.randint(0, n)
        queries.append([a, b, k])
    return [s, queries]
stress.note: narrow alphabet yields frequent palindromic ranges and ties
